creator: salvadore@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to Raphael Kubo da Costa from comment #4)

Yes and no.

It turned out that upnp-1.6.* and upnp-1.8.* are two distinct and incompatible branches of the project, both in development: this is similar to the python27/36 issue.

Thus,

- I've reverted original upnp to 1.6.25, applying to it the rest of the patch. I tested the dependent ports with upnp built with default options and it worked;
- I've created a new port named upnp18 to track the upnp-1.8.* development branch. I tested the build and it worked.

However I have not submitted the patch for 1.6.25 nor the new upnp18 port yet because I still have to solve the conflict between them: I think it is an easy fix and I hope to have it ready in a few days.
